instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: Referencing other constructors can be done with the keyword this

runs.text: Referencing other constructors can be done with the keyword

text_blocks.text: The most common functions are getters and setters
Getters retrieve the values of instance variables and return them

runs.text: Getters retrieve the values of instance variables and return them

text_blocks.text: Setters alter the values of the instance variables

runs.text: Setters alter the values of the instance variables

text_blocks.text: Why use these functions rather than setting the variable to be public?

runs.text: Why use these functions rather than setting the variable to be public?

text_blocks.text: Having these methods ensure you have full control over the instance variables

runs.text: Having these methods ensure you have full control over the instance variables

text_blocks.text: Should you decide to change how the variable is accessed or altered, you only need to change the method.

runs.text: Should you decide to change how the variable is accessed or altered, you only need to change the method.

text_blocks.text: You only add them when you need them!

runs.text: You only add them when you need them!

text_blocks.text: Building a single class is useless.  It needs to be used in context.

runs.text: Building a single class is useless.  It needs to be used in context.

text_blocks.text: There are two main relationships between classes

runs.text: There are two main relationships between classes

text_blocks.text: A subclass of a class is a more specific type of a class

runs.text: of a class is a more specific type of a class

text_blocks.text: Also known as the child of a class

runs.text: Also known as the child of a class

text_blocks.text: For example, a tree would be a subclass of plant

runs.text: For example, a tree would be a subclass of plant

text_blocks.text: Subclasses are meant to expand upon the behavior of their more general parent class

runs.text: Subclasses are meant to expand upon the behavior of their more general parent class

text_blocks.text: On the converse, a superclass is a more abstract and general version of a class

runs.text: is a more abstract and general version of a class

text_blocks.text: Also known as the parent of a class

runs.text: Also known as the parent of a class

text_blocks.text: Plant is the superclass of Tree
You can only have one superclass

input.title: Declaring a Class to be a Subtype

text_blocks.text: Declaring a Class to be a Subtype

text_blocks.text: In order to declare a class’s parent, use the keyword extends

text_blocks.text: (Classes that do not have a parent class implicitly extend the Object class)

runs.text: (Classes that do not have a parent class implicitly extend the Object class)

text_blocks.text: A subclass inherits all non-private fields and methods
Accessing these fields and methods requires the keyword super.

runs.text: Accessing these fields and methods requires the keyword

text_blocks.text: (Note: you may also be able to access private fields with the parent class’s getters and setters, which may be safer)

runs.text: (Note: you may also be able to access private fields with the parent class’s getters and setters, which may be safer)

text_blocks.text: What if you want a field or method to be inherited, but still do not necessarily want it to be accessed by anyone?

runs.text: What if you want a field or method to be inherited, but still do not necessarily want it to be accessed by anyone?

text_blocks.text: Remember, releasing your access to these fields and methods should only be done when necessary

runs.text: Remember, releasing your access to these fields and methods should only be done when necessary

text_blocks.text: Example A totally ignores the move method (essentially its precursor), but is clearer

runs.text: Example A totally ignores the move method (essentially its precursor), but is clearer

text_blocks.text: Example B shows the movement lineage

runs.text: Example B shows the movement lineage

text_blocks.text: Either is okay, but both need comments!!!!

runs.text: Either is okay, but both need comments!!!!

text_blocks.text: Constructors cannot be inherited, even though they are public

runs.text: Constructors cannot be inherited, even though they are public

text_blocks.text: However, you can, and should, use them from the subclass’s constructor, to ensure complete initialization

runs.text: However, you can, and should, use them from the subclass’s constructor, to ensure complete initialization

text_blocks.text: You can call the parent’s constructor by using the keyword super

text_blocks.text: Unlike calling an overloaded constructor of the same class, calling a superclass constructor does not need to be the only line of code

constructor does not need to be the only line of code

text_blocks.text: However, it must still be the first line of code in the constructor

input.title: What If the Superclass’s Method Definition Does Not Fit the Class’s?

text_blocks.text: What If the Superclass’s Method Definition Does Not Fit the Class’s?

text_blocks.text: As an example, our Car class should probably use the original “move” method, rather than creating an entirely new “drive” method that does something similar

runs.text: As an example, our Car class should probably use the original “move” method, rather than creating an entirely new “drive” method that does something similar

text_blocks.text: Unless your overridden method totally discards the functionality of the superclass’s method, you should call the superclass’s method at least once

text_blocks.text: Ownership is simply one class using another as a property

runs.text: Ownership is simply one class using another as a property

text_blocks.text: A Car may have an Engine, a Radio, and an Owner, depending on what you need
